---
title: "aa_within_condition_binomial — Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy — occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-06
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        table td, table th { padding-top: 0.5em; padding-bottom: 0.5em; }
        </style>
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
        \renewcommand{\arraystretch}{1.5}
tbl-colwidths: [15, 5, 12, 35, 12, 21]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![BWM 3x3 grid of unweighted amino-acid binomial volcano panels: rows are the three BWM timepoints (top to bottom: Day 0, Day 5, Day 10); columns are ribosome sites in A / P / E order (left to right). Each panel plots -log10(FDR) on the y-axis against Log2 Enrichment on the x-axis (x range ~-1.0 to +1.0); each glyph is one of the 20 amino acids labelled by its one-letter code and coloured by chemical property (red = acidic, blue = basic, green = hydrophobic, orange = neutral, purple = polar). Triangles mark FDR<0.05 cells and circles mark non-significant cells; the dotted vertical line is Log2 Enrichment = 0, the dashed vertical lines mark +/-0.5, and the dashed horizontal line marks FDR = 0.05.](../plots/within_condition/composite/unweighted/BWM_volcano_grid.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

![control 3x3 grid of unweighted amino-acid binomial volcano panels: rows are the three control timepoints (top to bottom: Day 0, Day 5, Day 10); columns are ribosome sites in A / P / E order. Axes, glyph labelling, colour key, and dashed reference lines are identical to the BWM grid above.](../plots/within_condition/composite/unweighted/control_volcano_grid.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each glyph is one amino acid tested against its transcriptome background frequency (`bg_freq`) in that group; a panel is one (group, site) BH-FDR family of 20 amino acids. A glyph to the right of zero is occupied more often than its transcriptomic abundance predicts in that group, to the left less often, and higher up the panel means a smaller FDR. The test is **within-group**: BWM and control are never compared directly here — that contrast lives in the per-timepoint Fisher sister file. Panels are arranged in A / P / E ribosome-site order across columns.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `aa_within_condition_binomial.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `within_condition_binomial`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `codon_within_condition_binomial.csv`
- **Test:** One-sample binomial test (`observed_count` out of `total_n` vs H0: `observed_freq` = `bg_freq`, two-sided); BH-FDR within each (group, site) family of 20 amino acids
- **Design:** Per (group, site, aa) one-sample binomial: the observed `observed_count` is tested against the binomial null `Binomial(total_n, bg_freq)`, where `bg_freq` is the amino-acid frequency in the transcriptome composition. 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10) x 3 sites (A/P/E) x 20 amino acids = 360 tests; replicates are pooled before the test (pseudoreplicated); BH-FDR is applied independently within each of 18 (group, site) families of 20 amino acids. The test is **within-group**: each group is tested against its own transcriptome background, never against another group.

### Family-level caveats

- **pseudorep** — replicates within a group are summed before `binomtest`. Standard pseudoreplication; anti-conservative.
- **iid-violation-binomial** — the binomial test assumes `total_n` iid Bernoulli draws with probability p_bg; ribosome footprints are not iid (one transcript contributes overlapping A/P/E windows, and transcripts vary widely in coverage). Headline-grade tiny p-values (here `p_adj` underflows to exactly 0.0 for 188 of 360 cells; smallest nonzero 9.39e-323) reflect this violation more than a per-feature biological signal. `log2_enrichment` is the primary effect column, not p.
- **bh-per-(group,site)** — BH-FDR is per (group,site) family of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon) tests. `p_adj` means "corrected within this group at this site", not across the whole CSV.
- **bg-transcriptome-freq** — the null frequency is the transcriptome codon/AA composition (the `Transcriptome` column), so enrichment means "more occupied than its transcriptomic abundance predicts", not vs a uniform expectation.

---

## Headline

Within-group binomial against `bg_freq`, 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10) x 3 sites (A/P/E) x 20 aa = 360 tests; BH-FDR per (group, site) family of 20. 349/360 hits at `p_adj` < 0.05 (350/360 at `p_adj` < 0.10). File min `p_adj` underflows to exactly 0.0: 188 of 360 cells return `p_adj` = 0.0 and the smallest nonzero `p_adj` is 9.39e-323 (control_day_5, E:R, `log2_enrichment` = +0.148, `observed_count` = 137894, `total_n` = 2414916). At whole-transcriptome `total_n` (~2.1-2.4M) FDR significance is near-universal: every one of the 60 (site, aa) cells clears the `#sig` >= 2 floor, so significance separates almost nothing and ranking is by magnitude and reproducibility instead. The dominant structure of this file is **cross-group concordance**: of the 60 (site, aa) cells, 20 are concordantly enriched and 26 concordantly depleted across all 6 groups, with 14 discordant; the largest-magnitude cells hold one direction across all 6 groups (E:C -0.77, E:K +0.64, P:D +0.58, P:W -0.57; the largest discordant cell A:W spans only +0.56 to -0.13). See the three cross-group sub-tables in Top hits. `p_adj` magnitudes are co-amplified by `iid-violation-binomial` (within-transcript correlation of overlapping A/P/E windows breaks the binomial independence assumption) and by common-aa `bg_freq` tightness; magnitude-plus-reproducibility is the anchor for reading.

## Top hits

The effect column is `log2_enrichment` = log2(observed `observed_freq` / `bg_freq`); positive means the amino acid is over-represented at that site in that group relative to its transcriptome abundance, negative under-represented. Top hits are three **cross-group** sub-tables that partition the 60 (site, aa) cells by the sign of `log2_enrichment` across the 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10): concordant enrichment (positive in all 6), concordant depletion (negative in all 6), and discordant (>= 1 sign disagreement). A cell enters the partition only if it has a value in all 6 groups (all 60 do). Each sub-table shows every cell that reached `p_adj` < 0.05 in at least 2 of the 6 groups, a reproducibility floor on the `#sig` axis (`#sig` = groups with FDR<0.05, of 6), **not** a fixed row cap; here every cell clears it, so the three tables show all 20 / 26 / 14 cells. Rows are sorted by site (A / P / E), then `#sig` descending, then `min count` (smallest `observed_count` across the 6 groups) descending. Ranking is deliberately not by p magnitude: `iid-violation-binomial` and common-aa `bg_freq` tightness inflate p for modest effects at this `total_n` (188 cells underflow to `p_adj` = 0.0). In the `log2_enrichment` column the six values are listed on two lines — **BWM** day_0, day_5, day_10 on the first line, then **control** day_0, day_5, day_10 on the second. The `flag` column carries `iid-amp` (a group `p_adj` < 1e-10) and `bg-tight` (the aa's `bg_freq` > 0.05, a tight binomial null); it would carry `rare-aa` for any cell below 100 counts, but none qualify here (the smallest `min count` is 9188 at P:W).

```{=latex}
\needspace{20\baselineskip}
```

### Concordant enrichment — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| Site | amino acid | log2_enrichment | min count | #sig | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | E (glutamate) | +0.25, +0.26, +0.39 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.30, +0.43, +0.39 | 109935 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | K (lysine) | +0.32, +0.28, +0.24 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.33, +0.44, +0.20 | 92136 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | D (aspartate) | +0.13, +0.39, +0.35 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.31, +0.38, +0.32 | 84720 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | G (glycine) | +0.35, +0.12, +0.27 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.32, +0.10, +0.26 | 82132 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | R (arginine) | +0.23, +0.21, +0.24 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.17, +0.14, +0.23 | 76722 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | Y (tyrosine) | +0.40, +0.25, +0.15 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.31, +0.31, +0.33 | 51487 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | D (aspartate) | +0.58, +0.52, +0.63 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.46, +0.60, +0.66 | 107811 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | E (glutamate) | +0.03, +0.08, +0.22 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.14, +0.19, +0.23 | 98276 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | K (lysine) | +0.28, +0.27, +0.17 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.36, +0.17, +0.20 | 92348 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | G (glycine) | +0.56, +0.21, +0.35 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.43, +0.21, +0.38 | 89273 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | N (asparagine) | +0.20, +0.28, +0.19 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.19, +0.32, +0.27 | 74656 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | Y (tyrosine) | +0.10, +0.17, +0.04 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.18, +0.15, +0.15 | 45463 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | V (valine) | +0.07, +0.06, +0.08 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.05, +0.18, +0.00 | 79150 | 5/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | K (lysine) | +0.65, +0.57, +0.53 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.69, +0.78, +0.63 | 124020 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | E (glutamate) | +0.19, +0.24, +0.26 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.26, +0.35, +0.30 | 103049 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | D (aspartate) | +0.15, +0.18, +0.21 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.18, +0.23, +0.23 | 79679 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | G (glycine) | +0.23, +0.04, +0.16 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.27, +0.16, +0.14 | 75866 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | R (arginine) | +0.10, +0.05, +0.08 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.07, +0.15, +0.08 | 68860 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | N (asparagine) | +0.08, +0.13, +0.10 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.13, +0.24, +0.13 | 67803 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| E | Q (glutamine) | +0.04, +0.10, +0.11 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.07, +0.13, +0.08 | 54903 | 6/6 | iid-amp |

: Concordant enrichment — all 6 groups enriched; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, sorted by site (A / P / E), then #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,20,37,9,7,21]"}

```{=latex}
\needspace{22\baselineskip}
```

### Concordant depletion — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| Site | amino acid | log2_enrichment | min count | #sig | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | L (leucine) | -0.02, -0.11, -0.19 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.17, -0.28, -0.13 | 99131 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | A (alanine) | -0.06, -0.14, -0.15 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.09, -0.32, -0.06 | 77106 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | S (serine) | -0.43, -0.41, -0.37 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.48, -0.44, -0.40 | 76314 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | T (threonine) | -0.32, -0.26, -0.31 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.30, -0.26, -0.31 | 60386 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | F (phenylalanine) | -0.29, -0.20, -0.19 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.26, -0.21, -0.17 | 52902 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| A | C (cysteine) | -0.29, -0.40, -0.34 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.48, -0.38, -0.31 | 21153 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| A | P (proline) | -0.01, -0.15, -0.23 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.11, -0.47, -0.11 | 57760 | 5/6 | iid-amp |
| P | L (leucine) | -0.36, -0.20, -0.27 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.29, -0.24, -0.39 | 82488 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | A (alanine) | -0.10, -0.19, -0.12 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.14, -0.18, -0.11 | 74517 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | S (serine) | -0.48, -0.39, -0.40 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.49, -0.40, -0.45 | 73823 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | T (threonine) | -0.28, -0.23, -0.24 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.23, -0.26, -0.22 | 64081 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | P (proline) | -0.15, -0.22, -0.17 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.09, -0.32, -0.13 | 56757 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | Q (glutamine) | -0.20, -0.19, -0.23 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.22, -0.38, -0.26 | 43566 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | M (methionine) | -0.33, -0.18, -0.23 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.20, -0.24, -0.27 | 25872 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | C (cysteine) | -0.46, -0.39, -0.35 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.47, -0.36, -0.30 | 21371 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | W (tryptophan) | -0.52, -0.51, -0.49 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.52, -0.73, -0.63 | 9188 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| E | L (leucine) | -0.19, -0.13, -0.10 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.25, -0.20, -0.18 | 95467 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | S (serine) | -0.54, -0.35, -0.31 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.50, -0.43, -0.39 | 77000 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | A (alanine) | -0.11, -0.16, -0.12 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.14, -0.33, -0.13 | 73460 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | I (isoleucine) | -0.05, -0.05, -0.12 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.06, -0.12, -0.09 | 73335 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | T (threonine) | -0.25, -0.19, -0.22 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.22, -0.33, -0.20 | 64954 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | F (phenylalanine) | -0.29, -0.23, -0.30 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.36, -0.43, -0.35 | 46767 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| E | Y (tyrosine) | -0.16, -0.07, -0.21 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.16, -0.29, -0.24 | 34659 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| E | H (histidine) | -0.16, -0.03, -0.04 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.05, -0.10, -0.07 | 27661 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| E | C (cysteine) | -0.78, -0.71, -0.67 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.87, -0.78, -0.82 | 14868 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| E | W (tryptophan) | -0.21, -0.27, -0.13 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.24, -0.09, -0.24 | 11982 | 6/6 | iid-amp |

: Concordant depletion — all 6 groups depleted; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, sorted by site (A / P / E), then #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,20,37,9,7,21]"}

```{=latex}
\needspace{22\baselineskip}
```

### Discordant — significant in >= 2 of 6 groups

| Site | amino acid | log2_enrichment | min count | #sig | flag |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| A | V (valine) | -0.11, -0.02, -0.02 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.01, -0.05, -0.04 | 76622 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | I (isoleucine) | -0.22, -0.03, -0.02 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.05, +0.12, -0.07 | 74556 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| A | N (asparagine) | -0.30, -0.06, -0.06 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.14, +0.08, -0.26 | 51669 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| A | Q (glutamine) | +0.01, -0.02, -0.09 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.05, -0.08, -0.22 | 44639 | 5/6 | iid-amp |
| A | M (methionine) | -0.19, -0.01, +0.01 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.13, +0.09, -0.04 | 30255 | 5/6 | iid-amp |
| A | H (histidine) | +0.06, +0.03, -0.03 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.03, -0.02, -0.01 | 28939 | 5/6 | iid-amp |
| A | W (tryptophan) | +0.56, +0.03, -0.01 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.12, -0.13, +0.21 | 16363 | 5/6 | iid-amp |
| P | F (phenylalanine) | -0.08, -0.04, -0.07 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.15, +0.05, -0.10 | 55608 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| P | R (arginine) | +0.13, +0.08, +0.10 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.08, -0.00, +0.13 | 71588 | 5/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | I (isoleucine) | -0.06, +0.01, -0.14 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.03, +0.08, -0.16 | 69923 | 5/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| P | H (histidine) | +0.02, +0.10, -0.01 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.05, -0.06, +0.01 | 29167 | 4/6 | iid-amp |
| E | V (valine) | +0.08, +0.05, +0.04 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.04, -0.02, +0.03 | 80454 | 6/6 | iid-amp, bg-tight |
| E | P (proline) | +0.20, -0.11, -0.17 <br>`\newline`{=latex} +0.02, -0.31, -0.07 | 59507 | 6/6 | iid-amp |
| E | M (methionine) | -0.12, -0.04, +0.01 <br>`\newline`{=latex} -0.07, +0.16, -0.02 | 30636 | 5/6 | iid-amp |

: Discordant — at least one sign disagreement across the 6 groups; cells significant in >= 2 of 6 groups, sorted by site (A / P / E), then #sig then min count. {tbl-colwidths="[6,20,37,9,7,21]"}

### Flag glossary

- `iid-amp` — short for "**i.i.d.-violation amplified**" (i.i.d. = independent and identically distributed; see the `iid-violation-binomial` caveat under Family-level caveats). Applied to every Top-hits row where at least one group has `p_adj` < 1e-10. The binomial test assumes every counted position is an independent coin-flip, but footprints on the same transcript move together — so the test acts as if it has far more independent evidence than it does, and even a modest enrichment gets an astronomically tiny p. The flag is a reminder that this row's p looks tiny because of the broken independence assumption, not because the effect is biologically large. Read it by `log2_enrichment` + reproducibility, never by how small the p is.
- `bg-tight` — short for "**background-frequency tight**," meaning a narrow null. Applied to rows for a common amino acid (`bg_freq` > 0.05). For a common residue counted over hundreds of thousands of positions, the range of counts the binomial expects purely by chance (its null distribution) is very narrow, so even a small departure from `bg_freq` lands far out in the tail and produces a tiny p. Same upshot as `iid-amp`: the small p reflects how sharp the null is at large n, not a large biological effect. The two flags co-occur on common-amino-acid rows with extreme p.

## Numbers at a glance
- `n_tests`: 360 (6 groups x 3 sites x 20 aa)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 349
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 350
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.0 (underflow) - 188 of 360 cells return `p_adj` exactly 0.0; smallest nonzero `p_adj` is 9.39e-323 (control_day_5, E:R; log2 +0.148, k=137894, n=2414916). Under `iid-violation-binomial` + `bg-tight` this is not a magnitude claim.
- `p_floor`: n/a - no exact-test floor for the binomial at these n.
- Per-(group, site) hits at `p_adj` < 0.05 (each is 20 aa; A / P / E order; counts sum to 349):
  - BWM_day_0: A 18/20, P 20/20, E 20/20
  - BWM_day_5: A 20/20, P 19/20, E 20/20
  - BWM_day_10: A 18/20, P 19/20, E 19/20
  - control_day_0: A 20/20, P 20/20, E 20/20
  - control_day_5: A 20/20, P 19/20, E 20/20
  - control_day_10: A 19/20, P 18/20, E 20/20

## Biological interpretation

**The dominant signal of this file is cross-group concordance, not perturbation (concrete).** Of the 60 (site, amino acid) cells, 20 are concordantly enriched and 26 concordantly depleted across all 6 groups (BWM and control x d0/d5/d10), leaving 14 discordant — the partition feeding the three Top-hits tables. The largest-magnitude cells hold one direction across all 6 groups (E:C -0.77, E:K +0.64, P:D +0.58, P:W -0.57), while the largest discordant cell (A:W) spans only +0.56 to -0.13. Because the test is within-group against each group's own transcriptome background, these counts are a map of stable site-occupancy preferences, not a BWM-vs-control or a time contrast. The per-(group, site) counts of FDR<0.05 cells run 18/20 to 20/20 (Numbers at a glance) with no group's amino-acid map standing apart. The file therefore reads as a structural map of which amino acids the ribosome occupies more or less than their transcriptome abundance predicts, stable across perturbation and time; perturbation contrasts live in the per-timepoint Fisher sister file.

**E-site lysine and P-site aspartate are the two strongest reproducible enrichments (concrete).** E:K is the single largest enriched cell (+0.53 to +0.78 across the 6 groups, `min count` 124020) and P:D the largest at the P site (+0.46 to +0.66, `min count` 107811). E-site lysine is a textbook ribosome-dwell anchor: the positively charged lysine side chain interacts electrostatically with the negatively charged exit tunnel as the nascent chain emerges, slowing the elongation cycle. Aspartate is concordantly enriched at all three sites (A, P, E) and glutamate likewise (A:E, P:E, E:E all in the concordant-enrichment set), so the acidic residues coherently mark higher occupancy — consistent with the slower peptidyl-transfer chemistry that acidic residues impose. The stability across BWM/control x d0/d5/d10 is what makes these concordance signals rather than perturbation signals.

**Small and short-side-chain residue depletion (alanine, serine, threonine) across all three sites is the structural counterpart to the enrichment story (concrete).** Serine is concordantly depleted at all three sites (A:S -0.37 to -0.48, P:S -0.39 to -0.49, E:S -0.31 to -0.54), and alanine and threonine join it (alanine modest but consistently negative at A/P/E; threonine ~-0.2 to -0.3 at all three sites), each FDR<0.05 in every group with `min count` >= 60000. Alanine has the smallest non-glycine side chain and decodes through abundant tRNA isoacceptors, so it is mechanistically the most dwell-resistant residue; the small hydroxyl residues serine and threonine behave similarly. The uniform under-representation mirrors the K/D/E enrichment story by inversion — occupancy concentrates on residues with dwell-promoting side-chain chemistry (basic, acidic) and thins on the small, fast-decoding residues least likely to pause.

**The headline-grade p magnitudes (underflow to 0.0) are iid-violation amplification, not biological strength (concrete).** 188 of 360 cells return `p_adj` exactly 0.0 and the smallest nonzero is 9.39e-323 (control_day_5 E:R, `log2_enrichment` only +0.148). At `total_n` ~2.1-2.4M the binomial p collapses toward zero for any non-trivial enrichment, because the null assumes that many independent Bernoulli draws but ribosome footprints are not independent (one transcript contributes overlapping A/P/E windows and transcripts vary in coverage) — the `iid-violation-binomial` caveat. Combined with the `bg-tight` common-amino-acid nulls, modest effects compress to vanishing p. Read this file by `log2_enrichment` magnitude and cross-group reproducibility, in that order; treat any `p_adj` < 1e-10 as flagged by `iid-amp` (and `bg-tight` for high-`bg_freq` cells) regardless of magnitude.

**The 14 discordant cells are mostly small-magnitude and near zero (exploratory).** The discordant set is where sign disagrees across the 6 groups, but every discordant cell except A:W sits within roughly +/-0.3 and most cluster near zero (A:V, A:I, A:H, P:R, P:I, E:V), so the disagreements are more consistent with sign noise around a near-zero true effect than with a reproducible group-specific switch. A:W is the lone large swing (+0.56 in BWM_day_0 down to -0.13 in control_day_5), but at `min count` 16363 it is the lowest-count cell in the discordant table and carries no rare-aa flag only because every group clears 100. Worth checking whether any discordant cell flips in a group-consistent way that the per-timepoint Fisher (BWM-vs-control) or the codon-resolution sister CSV also picks up; absent that, treat the discordance as small-cell sign noise, not biology. Exploratory only — not a hit.

**What to carry forward.** *Concrete*: the cross-group concordance set (20 concordantly enriched + 26 concordantly depleted of the 60 cells) as a structural map of site occupancy stable across perturbation and time; E:K and P:D as the strongest enriched cells; uniform small-residue depletion (alanine, serine, threonine) at A/P/E as the structural-baseline counterpart; acidic residues (D, E) over-represented at all three sites. *Exploratory*: the 14 discordant cells — direction unreliable, mostly near-zero, worth a cross-check in the codon sister and the per-timepoint Fisher. *Noise*: any per-cell p-magnitude reading below the iid-amplification floor — quote magnitude + reproducibility, not p. Cross-checks worth running in sister files: does the per-timepoint Fisher show BWM-vs-control divergence overlaid on any of these concordance cells (perturbation overlay on the structural map); does the within-condition binomial codon sister split any amino-acid-level concordance into a single-synonym story (refining the reading from "amino-acid property" toward "specific codon / tRNA").

## Methods

The test is a one-sample binomial (`observed_count` ~ Binomial(`total_n`, `bg_freq`), two-sided), with BH-FDR computed within each (group, site) family of 20 amino-acid tests (each of the 18 sub-families is corrected independently, not against the full 360). Effect column is `log2_enrichment` = log2(`observed_freq` / `bg_freq`); a count-weighted variant `weighted_log2_enrichment` is also present in the CSV but is not used for ranking here (the unweighted log2 is what the binomial p reflects). The null `bg_freq` is the transcriptome amino-acid composition, so a positive `log2_enrichment` means "occupied more often than its transcriptomic abundance predicts," not "more occupied than control." The test answers "is amino acid X observed at this site at a different frequency than its transcriptome `bg_freq` in the same group?", **not** "is X enriched in BWM relative to control" (that is the per-timepoint Fisher) and **not** "does the per-replicate frequency change between timepoints" (that is between-timepoint Wilcoxon). Counts (`observed_count`) and totals (`total_n`) are summed across replicates before the test, hence the family-level pseudoreplication caveat. Top hits are presented as three cross-group tables that partition the 60 (site, aa) cells by the sign of `log2_enrichment` across the 6 groups: concordant enrichment (positive in all 6 groups; 20 cells), concordant depletion (negative in all 6; 26 cells), and discordant (at least one sign disagreement; 14 cells). A cell enters the partition only if it has a value in all 6 groups. Each table shows every cell that reached `p_adj` < 0.05 in at least 2 of the 6 groups (`#sig` >= 2, a reproducibility floor rather than a fixed row cap; all 60 cells clear it), the kept cells sorted by site (A / P / E), then `#sig` descending, then `min count` (smallest `observed_count` across the 6 groups) descending. Ranking is by magnitude and reproducibility, not p, because at `total_n` ~2.1-2.4M the binomial p collapses toward zero (188/360 underflow to exactly 0.0) for modest effects.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **pseudorep.** The binomial pools replicates into one `observed_count` and one `total_n` per (group, site, aa) cell; per-replicate variation is invisible to the statistic, so the smallest p magnitudes are inflated, not additional certainty.
- **iid-violation-binomial.** The binomial null assumes the `total_n` counted positions are i.i.d. (independent and identically distributed), but footprints on the same transcript are correlated and transcripts vary in coverage; this is the dominant reason p-values compress toward zero (188/360 to exactly 0.0). Rank by `log2_enrichment` + `observed_count` + cross-group reproducibility, not by p magnitude.
- **bh-per-(group, site).** BH-FDR is per (group, site) family of 20, so FDR control is within each 20-aa family, not pooled across the 60 amino acids per group or across the merged A/P/E file. Two rows with the same raw p in different (group, site) families can land at different `p_adj`.
- **bg-transcriptome-freq.** The null is the transcriptome amino-acid composition, so "enrichment" means "occupied more than its transcriptomic abundance predicts," not vs a uniform expectation or vs control. A positive `log2_enrichment` is a within-group abundance-relative readout.

### Considered and ruled out

- **rare-aa.** Checked for low-count instability (the checker flags any cell below 100 counts in any group); raised but ruled out — the smallest `min count` across all 60 cells is 9188 (P:W), far above the threshold, so no amino-acid cell is low-count and the flag fires on no row. (The sister codon CSV does carry a rare-codon flag, but only on the stop codon TGA.)

---

## Plots — individual

Each panel below is one (group, site) BH-FDR family of 20 amino acids, plotted as a volcano: `log2_enrichment` (x-range ~-1.0 to +1.0) on the x-axis, -log10(FDR) on the y-axis. Triangles mark FDR<0.05; circles are non-significant. Amino acids are labelled by one-letter code and coloured by chemical property (red = acidic, blue = basic, green = hydrophobic, orange = neutral, purple = polar). The horizontal dashed line marks FDR = 0.05; the vertical dashed lines mark log2 = +/-0.5. Most amino acids sit pinned near the top of each panel because the FDR underflows at whole-transcriptome n.

![BWM Day 0, A site. Largest depletion at S (serine, ~-0.43, top-left); largest enrichment at W (tryptophan, ~+0.56, far right). H (histidine, ~+0.06, -log10 FDR ~20) and L (leucine, ~0, -log10 FDR ~10) sit lower on the FDR axis; the remaining amino acids are pinned at the top.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 0, P site. Largest enrichments at D (aspartate, ~+0.58) and G (glycine, ~+0.55) on the right; largest depletions at W (tryptophan) and S (serine, ~-0.5). E (glutamate, -log10 FDR ~15) and H (histidine, -log10 FDR ~4) sit lower near log2 0.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 0, E site. K (lysine, ~+0.65, far right) is the largest enrichment; C (cysteine, ~-0.78, far left) the largest depletion, with S (serine, ~-0.54) next. Q (glutamine, -log10 FDR ~12) sits lower near log2 0; I (isoleucine) sits at -log10 FDR ~40.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_0_E_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5, A site. Largest enrichment at D (aspartate, ~+0.39); largest depletion at S (serine, ~-0.41). N (asparagine) is pinned high near log2 0 (-log10 FDR ~41); I (isoleucine, -log10 FDR ~15), V (valine), H (histidine), Q (glutamine), W (tryptophan), and M (methionine) trail down the log2-0 axis.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5, P site. D (aspartate, ~+0.52) is the largest enrichment (far right); W (tryptophan) and S (serine, ~-0.5) the largest depletions. F (phenylalanine, -log10 FDR ~19) sits lower near log2 0.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 5, E site. K (lysine, ~+0.57) is the largest enrichment; C (cysteine, ~-0.71) the largest depletion. V (valine), Y (tyrosine), I (isoleucine), R (arginine), and G (glycine) string down the log2-0 axis at decreasing FDR; M (methionine) and H (histidine) sit lowest.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_5_E_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10, A site. Largest enrichments at E (glutamate, ~+0.39) and D (aspartate, ~+0.35); largest depletion at S (serine, ~-0.37), with C (cysteine) and T (threonine) clustered nearby. N (asparagine) is pinned high near log2 0 (-log10 FDR ~33); I (isoleucine), V (valine), and H (histidine) sit lower.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_A_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10, P site. D (aspartate, ~+0.63) is the largest enrichment (far right); W (tryptophan) and S (serine, ~-0.49) the largest depletions. F (phenylalanine, pinned ~35) and Y (tyrosine, -log10 FDR ~9) sit lower near log2 0.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_P_volcano.png)

![BWM Day 10, E site. K (lysine, ~+0.53) is the largest enrichment; C (cysteine, ~-0.67) the largest depletion. W (tryptophan, -log10 FDR ~30) and V (valine, -log10 FDR ~19) sit lower near log2 0; H (histidine) sits lowest.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/BWM_day_10_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 0, A site. K (lysine, ~+0.33) is the largest enrichment, with D (aspartate), Y (tyrosine), E (glutamate), and G (glycine) clustered just to its left; S (serine, ~-0.49) and C (cysteine) the largest depletions. Q (glutamine, ~-0.05) sits notably lower (-log10 FDR ~39); H (histidine) and V (valine) sit near log2 0.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 0, P site. D (aspartate, ~+0.55) is the largest enrichment (G glycine ~+0.43 next); W (tryptophan) and S (serine, ~-0.52) the largest depletions. I (isoleucine, -log10 FDR ~27) and H (histidine, -log10 FDR ~19) sit lower near log2 0; the densest P-site panel in the file (20/20 hits).](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 0, E site. K (lysine, ~+0.69) is the largest enrichment and the file's most extreme cell; C (cysteine, ~-0.87) the largest depletion (file minimum log2), S (serine, ~-0.50) next. M (methionine), V (valine), H (histidine), and P (proline) trail down near log2 0.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_0_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 5, A site. K (lysine, ~+0.44) and the acidic residues E (glutamate, ~+0.43) and D (aspartate, ~+0.38) on the right are the largest enrichments; S (serine) and P (proline, ~-0.47) the largest depletions. V (valine, -log10 FDR ~35) and H (histidine) sit lower near log2 0.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 5, P site. D (aspartate, ~+0.60) is the largest enrichment; W (tryptophan, ~-0.73) the largest depletion and the P-site maximum magnitude in the file. F (phenylalanine, -log10 FDR ~36) and H (histidine, -log10 FDR ~25) sit lower near log2 0.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 5, E site. K (lysine, ~+0.78) is the largest enrichment and the file maximum log2; C (cysteine, ~-0.78) the largest depletion. W (tryptophan, ~-0.09, -log10 FDR ~26) and V (valine, -log10 FDR ~7) sit lower near log2 0.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_5_E_volcano.png)

![control Day 10, A site. E (glutamate, ~+0.39) is the largest enrichment, with D (aspartate) and Y (tyrosine, ~+0.32) next; S (serine, ~-0.40) the largest depletion, with C (cysteine), T (threonine), and N (asparagine) clustered nearby. I (isoleucine) is pinned high near log2 0 (-log10 FDR ~42); A (alanine, -log10 FDR ~30), V (valine, -log10 FDR ~17), and M (methionine) sit lower.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_A_volcano.png)

![control Day 10, P site. D (aspartate, ~+0.66) is the largest enrichment (G glycine ~+0.38 next); W (tryptophan, ~-0.63) the largest depletion. The remaining amino acids are pinned near the top across the log2 range (16/20 P-site hits).](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_P_volcano.png)

![control Day 10, E site. K (lysine, ~+0.63) is the largest enrichment; C (cysteine, ~-0.82) the largest depletion. Q (glutamine, -log10 FDR ~34) and P (proline, -log10 FDR ~30) sit lower; H (histidine, -log10 FDR ~16) and V (valine, -log10 FDR ~7) sit lowest near log2 0.](../plots/within_condition/individual/unweighted/control_day_10_E_volcano.png)
